coordinate_system: component-side board millimetres; source-image pixels are unscaled originals
status: D56 package and three-callout field registered but electrically held; D15 cut pair and the D14 local ground link photo-closed; D11 endpoints and remaining D14 paths pending

drawing.observation: The position-150 leader marks the sleeved upper conductor, while three position-159 leaders reach solder locations on one row beside D56. Assembly note 11 explicitly identifies position 150 as tubing fitted at solder locations, so neither number is interpreted as a cut instruction or a net change.

package_identity.orientation: component-view notch down; solder view reflected

component_observations.observation: independent upper-overlap view shows the same marked body, notch, adjacent resistor, board edge, and pin fanout
primary close view resolves the К155АГ3 8901 marking and notch-down package
independent lower-overlap view repeats the marking, notch, adjacent resistor, board edge, and pin fanout

solder_observations.observation: the reflected package field fixes the two package joints at the three-leader drawing level; the separate left landing and nearby rail stub are visible but not electrically assigned
overlap view independently repeats both package joints and the same three-location row

callout_field.result: The factory detail is registered to the D56.12/D56.5 physical level, but the position-150 tubing and position-159 solder callouts do not by themselves prove a connection between those pins, the separate left landing, or the adjacent rail.

callout_identity_evidence.observation: Assembly note 11 says to fit tubing positions 157 and 150 at solder locations; this disproves the former position-150-equals-cut interpretation. The specification identity of position 159 was not photographed, so it remains a guarded solder-location callout rather than an inferred material or conductor.

d56.remaining_boundary: Map the separate left landing and all conductors at the registered D56.12/D56.5 level with direct continuity or a complete item-159 specification. The visible nearby wide-rail gap is not promoted as the position-150 operation or as proof of the D56.12 net partition.

drawing.observation: The four-hole auxiliary trace beside D15 is cut between its final two holes, aligned between visible package-pad levels 8 and 9. No replacement wire is drawn in this D15 detail.

solder_confirmation.observation: short diagonal solder-side copper reaches D15.8
short diagonal solder-side copper reaches D15.9

d15.cut_observation: Two independent component photographs show the copper removed between the upper and lower auxiliary annuli. The cut separates the original A2/A1 bridge; the clean source PCB already assigns D15.8=A2 and D15.9=A1 as distinct nets.
d15.fabrication_boundary: The locally registered landing centres are evidence for topology, not drill placement. Reproducing the original auxiliary holes requires a direct board measurement or a fabrication-grade local scale.

drawing.observation: With the notch-up package orientation fixed by both component photographs, the drawing's four-hole right row is D14.8 through D14.5 and the first four holes of its five-hole left row are D14.1 through D14.4. The fifth left-row hole is an auxiliary landing below D14.4. The position-159 leader reaches the short D14.1-side stub joining the inter-package trace.

component_observations.observation: one uninterrupted vertical component-side copper strip joins the adjacent D32.4 and D14.1 annuli
independent close view shows the same uninterrupted inter-package copper strip

ground_link.result: The executed position-159 topology ties D14.1 to the same GND net as D32.4. The clean source PCB must assign D14.1 to GND.

d14.remaining_boundary: The row identity does not yet prove the remote endpoints of the fifth auxiliary landing, the three long drawn traces, or the right-row dogleg. Position 159 marks solder locations and does not prove these are replacement conductors. D14.2 and D14.7 therefore remain explicit measurement boundaries, and no auxiliary drill geometry is promoted.

drawing.observation: The unique L-shaped trace and the adjacent drilled column register directly to the component-side owner photographs. The long column is an auxiliary drilled/copper field beside D11, not a drawn 14-pad package column. Position 159 points to the right-hand junction of a four-landing subfield: an upper landing reaches it by a long vertical trace, a left landing approaches through the obscured horizontal bridge, and a lower landing leaves on a separate trace.

solder_exclusion.observation: The held-out-validated reflected package fit localizes a conspicuous solder scar beside D11 pins 4 through 6, but cross-registration places the factory drawing's component-side four-landing field elsewhere. The scar is therefore not evidence for the position-159 bridge and must not be used to assign its pins or nets.

d11.remaining_boundary: The four physical landing identities and the position-159 junction are now registered in two component views. The component-grid board fit is not pin- or fabrication-grade here, the corresponding solder-side holes are not locally registered, and the obscured bridge does not prove a D11 pin/net or either remote trace endpoint. Preserve the source nets and require a local through-hole fit or direct continuity before promoting replacement copper.
